title: Dennis Hallman

infobox.value: Dennis Lloyd Hallman  (1975-12-02) December 2, 1975 (age 50) [Olympia, Washington](/wiki/Olympia,_Washington "Olympia, Washington"), U.S.

2nd degree black belt in [Brazilian jiu-jitsu](/wiki/Brazilian_jiu-jitsu "Brazilian jiu-jitsu") under [Fabiano Scherner](/wiki/Fabiano_Scherner "Fabiano Scherner")[[1]](#cite_note-1)[[2]](#cite_note-liprofile-2)

(born December 2, 1975) is an American former professional [mixed martial artist](/wiki/Mixed_martial_artist "Mixed martial artist") who competed from 1996 to 2015. A veteran of 75 fights, he competed in various promotions including the [UFC](/wiki/Ultimate_Fighting_Championship "Ultimate Fighting Championship"), [Strikeforce](/wiki/Strikeforce_(mixed_martial_arts) "Strikeforce (mixed martial arts)"), [IFL](/wiki/International_Fight_League "International Fight League"), the [World Series of Fighting](/wiki/World_Series_of_Fighting "World Series of Fighting"), [Titan FC](/wiki/Titan_Fighting_Championship "Titan Fighting Championship"), [Shooto](/wiki/Shooto "Shooto"), and [King of the Cage](/wiki/King_of_the_Cage "King of the Cage"). He is most notable for infamously sporting a blue speedo to the octagon in his UFC fight against [Brian Ebersole](/wiki/Brian_Ebersole "Brian Ebersole"). He is also notable for being the only fighter other than [George St. Pierre](/wiki/George_St._Pierre "George St. Pierre") and [B.J. Penn](/wiki/B.J._Penn "B.J. Penn") to defeat former [UFC Welterweight Champion](/wiki/UFC_Welterweight_Championship "UFC Welterweight Championship") [Matt Hughes](/wiki/Matt_Hughes_(fighter) "Matt Hughes (fighter)") twice.

**Dennis Lloyd Hallman** (born December 2, 1975) is an American former professional [mixed martial artist](/wiki/Mixed_martial_artist "Mixed martial artist") who competed from 1996 to 2015. A veteran of 75 fights, he competed in various promotions including the [UFC](/wiki/Ultimate_Fighting_Championship "Ultimate Fighting Championship"), [Strikeforce](/wiki/Strikeforce_(mixed_martial_arts) "Strikeforce (mixed martial arts)"), [IFL](/wiki/International_Fight_League "International Fight League"), the [World Series of Fighting](/wiki/World_Series_of_Fighting "World Series of Fighting"), [Titan FC](/wiki/Titan_Fighting_Championship "Titan Fighting Championship"), [Shooto](/wiki/Shooto "Shooto"), and [King of the Cage](/wiki/King_of_the_Cage "King of the Cage"). He is most notable for infamously sporting a blue speedo to the octagon in his UFC fight against [Brian Ebersole](/wiki/Brian_Ebersole "Brian Ebersole"). He is also notable for being the only fighter other than [George St. Pierre](/wiki/George_St._Pierre "George St. Pierre") and [B.J. Penn](/wiki/B.J._Penn "B.J. Penn") to defeat former [UFC Welterweight Champion](/wiki/UFC_Welterweight_Championship "UFC Welterweight Championship") [Matt Hughes](/wiki/Matt_Hughes_(fighter) "Matt Hughes (fighter)") twice.

## Background

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Dennis_Hallman&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Background")]

Hallman was born in [Olympia, Washington](/wiki/Olympia,_Washington "Olympia, Washington"), and began training when he was eight years old. He was on the [Wrestling](/wiki/Wrestling "Wrestling") team at [Yelm High School](/wiki/Yelm_High_School "Yelm High School") and won the 135-pound state championship in his senior year. Hallman had planned to continue his career at a community college, but broke his leg during the pre-season training, and had to be sidelined for the entire season. A former high school classmate was sending in video tapes to Battlecade and invited Hallman to fight him because Hallman was a well-known wrestler. Hallman won the fight by a [guillotine choke](/wiki/Guillotine_choke "Guillotine choke"), and his opponent's praise of Hallman's potential encouraged Hallman to pursue a career in MMA. Hallman had an exhibition match three weeks later against a Japanese fighter who was training with [Matt Hume](/wiki/Matt_Hume "Matt Hume"), which Hallman also won.[[3]](#cite_note-3)

## MMA career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Dennis_Hallman&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: MMA career")]

### Ultimate Fighting Championship

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Dennis_Hallman&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Ultimate Fighting Championship")]

Two of Hallman's biggest achievements are his wins (both by submission in a combined 38 seconds) over [UFC](/wiki/Ultimate_Fighting_Championship "Ultimate Fighting Championship") Hall of Famer and former welterweight champion [Matt Hughes](/wiki/Matt_Hughes_(fighter) "Matt Hughes (fighter)"). Their first fight was in 1998 at *Extreme Challenge 21*, the second in December 2000 at *[UFC 29: Defense of the Belts](/wiki/UFC_29 "UFC 29")*.

#### Title shot

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Dennis_Hallman&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Title shot")]

After his second victory over Matt Hughes, Hallman moved down in weight and fought Hughes' teammate [Jens Pulver](/wiki/Jens_Pulver "Jens Pulver") for the [lightweight](/wiki/Lightweight "Lightweight") [UFC](/wiki/Ultimate_Fighting_Championship "Ultimate Fighting Championship") championship in September 2001. Both Hallman and Pulver wrestled at the high school level in Washington. Hallman claimed to know Pulver since high school and expressed dislike toward the fighter. Although Pulver denied knowing Hallman personally, he knew of Hallman through their state champion wrestling careers (at different weight classes).

During the fight Hallman attempted an [armbar](/wiki/Armbar "Armbar") on Pulver (the same move he used to defeat Hughes in their second meeting), but Pulver countered the technique and followed with a left hook that landed flush on Hallman's chin, staggering him. Hallman was not able to recover completely from the blow, fighting very passively for the remainder of the bout (which eventually resulted in a unanimous decision loss). Hallman later stated in interviews that he simply wanted to survive the fight after receiving the blow.

### Return to the UFC

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Dennis_Hallman&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Return to the UFC")]

Hallman made his return to the UFC against [John Howard](/wiki/John_Howard_(fighter) "John Howard (fighter)") on December 5, 2009, at [The Ultimate Fighter 10 Finale](/wiki/The_Ultimate_Fighter:_Heavyweights_Finale "The Ultimate Fighter: Heavyweights Finale").[[4]](#cite_note-4) After controlling Howard on the ground for most of the bout, Hallman lost via KO at 4:55 of the third round.

Hallman then announced that he would try and move down to lightweight.[[5]](#cite_note-5)

Hallman then signed to face [Ben Saunders](/wiki/Ben_Saunders_(fighter) "Ben Saunders (fighter)") in a welterweight bout on August 7, 2010, at [UFC 117](/wiki/UFC_117 "UFC 117").[[6]](#cite_note-6) Hallman defeated Saunders via unanimous decision.

Hallman faced returning UFC veteran [Karo Parisyan](/wiki/Karo_Parisyan "Karo Parisyan") on November 20, 2010, at [UFC 123](/wiki/UFC_123 "UFC 123").[[7]](#cite_note-7) Hallman defeated Parisyan via TKO due to strikes in the first round after he dropped Parisyan with a stiff right, then finished with hammerfists.

Hallman was expected to face [TJ Waldburger](/wiki/TJ_Waldburger "TJ Waldburger") on March 26, 2011, at [UFC Fight Night 24](/wiki/UFC_Fight_Night_24 "UFC Fight Night 24").[[8]](#cite_note-8) However, Hallman was forced out of the bout with a knee injury and replaced by [Johny Hendricks](/wiki/Johny_Hendricks "Johny Hendricks").[[9]](#cite_note-9)

On January 15, 2011, it was announced that Hallman has extended his UFC contract for four additional UFC fights.[[10]](#cite_note-10)

Hallman faced [Brian Ebersole](/wiki/Brian_Ebersole "Brian Ebersole") on August 6, 2011, at [UFC 133](/wiki/UFC_133 "UFC 133").[[11]](#cite_note-11) He lost the fight via TKO in the first round. Wearing particularly skimpy blue shorts during the fight, in a ground battle Hallman's left testicle was briefly exposed, eliciting chants of "Put some clothes on" from the crowd and call for regulation of fighter shorts from UFC President [Dana White](/wiki/Dana_White "Dana White"). Hallman has since stated that he wore the shorts as a result of losing a bet.[[12]](#cite_note-12)

Hallman next faced [John Makdessi](/wiki/John_Makdessi "John Makdessi") on December 10, 2011, at [UFC 140](/wiki/UFC_140 "UFC 140").[[13]](#cite_note-13) In the lead up to the bout, Hallman missed the 156 pound weight limit by weighing in at 158.5, resulting in a fine and the fight being changed to a catchweight bout. Hallman defeated Makdessi via submission in the first round.

Hallman was expected to face [Tony Ferguson](/wiki/Tony_Ferguson "Tony Ferguson") on May 5, 2012, at [UFC on Fox 3](/wiki/UFC_on_Fox_3 "UFC on Fox 3").[[14]](#cite_note-14) However, Hallman was forced out of the bout with an injury and replaced by [Thiago Tavares](/wiki/Thiago_Tavares "Thiago Tavares").[[15]](#cite_note-15)

Hallman was expected to face [Thiago Tavares](/wiki/Thiago_Tavares "Thiago Tavares") on September 1, 2012, at [UFC 151](/wiki/UFC_151 "UFC 151").[[16]](#cite_note-16) However, after [UFC 151](/wiki/UFC_151 "UFC 151") was cancelled, Hallman/Tavares was rescheduled to take place on October 5, 2012, at [UFC on FX 5](/wiki/UFC_on_FX_5 "UFC on FX 5").[[17]](#cite_note-17) After the weigh-ins of [UFC on FX 5](/wiki/UFC_on_FX_5 "UFC on FX 5") Hallman was 7 lbs. over the 156 lb. weight limit and Tavares said if Hallman could make 3 lbs. over the weight limit he would agree to fight him but Hallman could not and the bout was scrapped from the card. Hallman said he missed weight because of personal issues at home and both him and Tavares were paid their show money. Hallman was released from the UFC for missing weight twice in a row.[[18]](#cite_note-18)

### World Series Of Fighting

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Dennis_Hallman&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: World Series Of Fighting")]

Hallman fought [Jon Fitch](/wiki/Jon_Fitch "Jon Fitch") on July 5, 2014, at [World Series of Fighting 11: Gaethje vs. Newell](/wiki/World_Series_of_Fighting_11:_Gaethje_vs._Newell "World Series of Fighting 11: Gaethje vs. Newell").[[19]](#cite_note-19) He lost the fight via unanimous decision.

### ProFC

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Dennis_Hallman&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: ProFC")]

Hallman faced with Russian Hand-to-hand champion and former Bellator MMA fighter [Michail Tsarev](/wiki/Michail_Tsarev "Michail Tsarev") on November 1, 2014. He lost the fight via TKO.

### League S-70

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Dennis_Hallman&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: League S-70")]

Hallman faced to Mikhail Kolobegov on August 29, 2015, at League S-70: Russia vs. World.[[20]](#cite_note-20) He lost the fight via TKO (punches).[[21]](#cite_note-21)

## Boxing career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Dennis_Hallman&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Boxing career")]

Hallman made his professional boxing debut on August 23, 2014, in [Tacoma, Washington](/wiki/Tacoma,_Washington "Tacoma, Washington"), at the [Emerald Queen Casino](/wiki/Emerald_Queen_Casino "Emerald Queen Casino") on the [Battle at the Boat](/wiki/Battle_at_the_Boat "Battle at the Boat") 97 card. As of May 2020, his professional boxing record stands at 1–3.[[22]](#cite_note-22)

## Personal life

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Dennis_Hallman&action=edit&section=10 "Edit section: Personal life")]

Hallman has three sons, Ryley, Richie, and Jayden and a daughter named Kylar, who was born in December 2008.[[23]](#cite_note-23)

He has [Celiac Disease](/wiki/Coeliac_Disease "Coeliac Disease"), an autoimmune digestive reaction to [gluten](/wiki/Gluten "Gluten"), which hindered his ability to recover properly from physical activities such as training and fighting.[[24]](#cite_note-24)

### Mixed martial arts

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Dennis_Hallman&action=edit&section=11 "Edit section: Mixed martial arts")]

* **[Ultimate Fighting Championship](/wiki/Ultimate_Fighting_Championship "Ultimate Fighting Championship")**
  + **[UFC Encyclopedia Awards](/wiki/List_of_UFC_encyclopedia_award_recipients "List of UFC encyclopedia award recipients")**
    - [Submission of the Night](/wiki/List_of_UFC_encyclopedia_award_recipients "List of UFC encyclopedia award recipients") (One time) vs. [Matt Hughes](/wiki/Matt_Hughes_(fighter) "Matt Hughes (fighter)")[[25]](#cite_note-25)

## Mixed martial arts record
